name: Chocolate Truffle Tart with Raspberries
recipeIngredient: Assumed 2 Raspberry pints (or approximately 3-4 cups of raspberries)
recipeInstructions:
1. Chocolate Pastry: Combine flour, sugar, and cocoa in a food processor bowl. Pulse for 2 or 3 times to aerate the mixture. Chop the butter into pieces and add to the flour. Process until the mixture forms coarse crumbs; do not overprocess. With the motor running, drop the whole egg (do not include the shell) through the feed tube. Process briefly without allowing the dough to come together, as this will result in a tough pastry.
2. Set aside at room temperature until the filling is prepared.
3. Truffle Filling: Place chopped chocolate in a medium bowl. Heat whipping cream over medium-high heat until boiling, then pour it over the chocolate and whisk until completely melted. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate until set. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C). Using your hands, work the chocolate pastry and press it into an 8-inch tart pan with a removable bottom, aiming for even thickness. Chill for 20 minutes.
4. Prick the bottom of the pastry with a fork, then bake in the preheated oven for 20 to 25 minutes. Allow cooling completely.
5. To Assemble: Gently remove the tart from the pan and place it on a platter. Spoon the truffle filling into the shell and smooth the surface. Arrange raspberries over the top in concentric circles. Serve at room temperature for the best flavor.
Anne's note: Avoid making this recipe on very hot days, as the filling may melt.
